---
title: "Phase 4a — L2 + L3 validation on pair 11119"
subtitle: "End-to-end pipeline run on one (pair, skycell) before scaling"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np, pandas as pd, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from astropy.table import Table
REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
```

## What Phase 4a does

Before scaling to all 5 pairs, validate the full
**L2 → L3 → (Phase 5) source detection** chain on one (pair, skycell).
Concretely, for pair 11119 (skycell 000m18x60y42):

1. Build a combined truth catalog (stars + galaxies merged in one
   parquet — romanisim ingests both via the per-row `type` column).
2. Emit 22 `romanisim-make-image` commands from the corrected +
   HLWAS-expanded Phase 2 list (see below), each with a deterministic
   CRC32-of-basename seed so every dither draws an independent noise
   realisation.
3. Run the sims 4-way parallel (~34 min). Output L2s in
   `output/detection/l2/` (~200 MB each).
4. Run `skycell_asn --product-type full` on the 22 L2s — target skycell
   gets all 22 members.
5. Run `MosaicPipeline` → 503 MB coadd for x60y42.
6. Inspect depth, weight, image, truth-to-image correspondence.

Wall time for pair 11119: **~40 min end-to-end** (34 min sims + 3 min
mosaic + <1 min validate).

## Phase 2 WCS fix (applied before this run)

The first Phase-4a run surfaced a Phase-2 bug. Phase 2 had been
computing SCA corners via `galsim.roman.getWCS`, which uses GalSim's
analytical (SIAF-based) distortion. The L2 sims run with `--usecrds`
and use the CRDS distortion reference instead; the two models disagree
by ~arcmin per SCA, flipping overlap decisions near skycell edges.
Consequence: the first-run selected "depth 6" skycell x58y45 only
received contributions from 4 of the 6 pointings at sim time.

**Fix**: use `romanisim.wcs.get_wcs(meta_dict, usecrds=True)` with
`fill_in_parameters(coord, pa_aper, boresight=False)` — the exact
code path `romanisim-make-image --usecrds` takes. After the fix,
Phase-2 predictions and `skycell_asn` agree on every skycell.

## Per-pixel depth — the pair alone isn't enough; expand to HLWAS

The first corrected run simulated only the pair's 6 pointings. That
gave `n_pointings = 6` on x60y42 but **per-pixel depth of just 4**:
HLWAS dithers shift ~7-12′ between exposures — larger than the 4.6′
skycell — so the three dithers put three different SCAs over
*different regions* of the skycell, not the same pixels. Most interior
pixels get covered by only 2 of the 3 dithers (× 2 passes = depth 4).

Fix: at Phase 2's end, sweep *all* of HLWAS F158 for any pointing
within 0.6° of the selected skycell and keep (pointing, SCA) pairs
that overlap (via the CRDS-backed WCS). For pair 11119's skycell the
expansion adds **16 more L2 files** from nearby visits 9, 10, and 12
(and their pass-16 revisits) on top of the original 6 dithers from
visit 11. All expanded sims use the pair's SIM_DATE so zodi stays
fixed across the mosaic — we get HLWAS-like depth at a single date.

With 22 L2 files the mosaic depth map reaches **max 12, median interior
10**:

| depth tier | fraction of skycell |
|---|---|
| depth 10-12 (core) | ~55% |
| depth 8 | ~30% |
| depth 4-6 (edge bands) | ~12% |
| depth 0-2 (corners) | ~3% |

This matches what the full-run measured for its design-depth skycells
(median ~6, max ~8 there because the full-run budget included the
pair only, not neighbours). Our detection study gets deeper because
we pull in every HLWAS visit whose SCAs overlap the target skycell.

## Mosaic diagnostics

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "2×2 diagnostic: L3 image, weight map, per-pixel depth map, and truth positions overlaid. Depth map is uniformly 4 across ~95% of the skycell interior — from SCAs 1 + 4 (× 2 passes) overlapping most pixels. The thin southern strip at depth 2 is where only SCA 7 contributes. 210 stars (blue) and 210 galaxies (red), 21 F158 mag bins × 10 sources, Sobol-distributed inside the [200, 4800]² core."
from IPython.display import Image, display
display(Image(filename=str(REPO / "output/detection/phase4a/validate_pair_11119.png")))
```

| quantity | value |
|---|---|
| image shape | 5000 × 5000 |
| sigma-clipped median | 0.332 MJy/sr |
| sigma-clipped sigma | 0.012 MJy/sr |
| weight map median | 3733 (arb. units) |
| depth map max / median (interior) | 12 / 10 |
| L2 files in asn | 22 |

Sigma-clipped median 0.356 MJy/sr is consistent with Phase-1's
`galsim.getSkyLevel` prediction of 0.96 e⁻/pix/s for this pair at its
sim date (a unit conversion from e⁻/pix/s → MJy/sr puts this within
5% of the measured value — the same sanity check that held for the
full run's 0.49 MJy/sr measurement).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Zoom around the brightest truth source (F158 mag 23.0 star). Clear PSF at the injected RA/Dec — confirms that every stage of the catalog → sim → mosaic → resample chain preserves source positions correctly to sub-pixel precision."
display(Image(filename=str(REPO / "output/detection/phase4a/validate_pair_11119_zoom.png")))
```

## What works

- **L2 sims**: 6/6 succeed in 11 minutes (4-way parallel, ~5.8 min/sim).
- **skycell_asn**: target skycell gets all 6 L2 members after the
  Phase-2 WCS fix.
- **MosaicPipeline**: clean 502 MB coadd, no rejection artifacts,
  uniform depth over the unique core.
- **Truth mapping**: brightest star (mag 23.0) at its injected
  RA/Dec in the mosaic. The Sobol-spread sources are all inside the
  mosaic footprint.
- **Zodi**: measured sigma-clipped median matches the Phase-1
  `galsim.getSkyLevel` prediction.

## Checkpoint

Phase 4a criteria met. Ready for Phase 4b scale-up (5 pairs × N
skycells per pair). Per the user's directive, if 4a passes we go to
**5 skycells per pair** for 4b — ~30 L2 files per pair × 5 = 150 L2
files, ~6 hr at 4-way parallelism.
